name: Montage: Beat 9: Breaking the Siege Line

text.content:
Beat 9: Breaking the Siege Line

Act: Act 1 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

Breaching the Ring of the Dead

Level: 3 | Beat: 9 | Location: Hrafnborg outskirts — the tannery marshes on the southwestern approach

Hrafnborg is encircled by thousands of silent undead standing shoulder to shoulder. The party must cross the siege ring and reach the city walls. The southwestern approach through the tannery marshes is the best route—but "best" is relative.

Difficulty: Hard (adjusted for 4 heroes + 2 NPC companions) | Rounds: 2

Tactical note from Lew: "The southwest, near the tannery ditches. The ground's soft—even dead legs don't like mud. The line's two deep there instead of three, and the wights are clustered near the gates, not the margins."

NPC Contributions:

  • Lew: Assists one PC's test per round (Intuition +4). His eye picks out gaps in the line, soft ground, moments when the dead shift and create openings.
  • Kaelen: Assists one PC's test per round (Might +3 or Presence +2). She can suppress her ember-mark's glow temporarily, or physically shield someone from a grasping hand.

Important: The dead are not under Grafvitnir's direct command here. They are drawn to the Ever-Ember like moths to a candle—compelled, not directed. This is a spiritual blockade, not a tactical one. None of them make a sound.

Outcomes

  • Total Success (5+ successes, 0 failures): Reach the postern gate undetected. Guards open it immediately—they recognize the King's cipher. Enter without combat. +1 Victory.
  • Partial Success (success limit reached with 1 failure): Reach the gate but with commotion. One PC takes 5 damage from grasping hands. Guards seal the gate with fire and oil afterward—Hrafnborg's defenses are weakened (−1 to Defence Track).
  • Total Failure (failure limit reached first): The postern route collapses. Running battle to the main gate—Harald orders a sortie to pull them in. Each PC takes 8 damage. The sortie costs 6 Hrafnborg guards. Harald's grief and anger sets the tone for the reunion. −2 to Defence Track.

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←15] (hard).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

Round 1: The Approach Through the Marsh

The party descends from the ridge, crosses open ground, and enters the tannery marshes. They must reach the edge of the siege ring without drawing the full host's attention.

Navigate the Marsh (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15])

Pick a path through frozen mud and tannery runoff without getting stuck or making noise. The ground that looks solid often isn't—and a thrashing party member draws attention.

Control Your Fear (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15])

The dead are right there. Suppress the panic and keep moving quietly. The silence of the siege ring—thousands of corpses making no sound—is worse than any battle cry.

Move Unseen (Quick or Discreet, modified [Resolute←15])

Slip between the bodies. Time your steps to the ring's slow, breathing-like sway. This isn't a gap—it's a momentary loosening you have seconds to exploit before the dead close back together.

Physical Endurance (Strong, modified [Resolute←15])

Crawl through freezing muck, lift stuck companions, push through reed banks without making sound. The marsh is unforgiving and the cold is absolute—every combat action is a contest against the terrain.

Read the Pattern (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15])

The dead shift in waves—like a crowd breathing. Predict the next gap and signal the group. Move before it closes. The wrong call means walking into a wall of corpses in the dark.

Suppress the Mark (Presence — Kaelen primarily)

Kaelen's ember-mark is drawing attention. The flame is so weak at the heart that it's pulling the dead toward warmth instead of pushing them away. Help her muffle the glow with mud, cloth, or sheer force of will—before the entire host turns.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

Round 2: The Final Sprint to the Walls

After the Frost-Ghoul combat, the party is close to the walls but exposed. The siege ring has shifted—the gap near the tannery waste-gate is closing.

Sprint Through the Gap (Strong, modified [Resolute←15])

Raw speed and endurance through freezing mud while the dead close in. The gap is yards wide and closing. There's no elegant solution—just legs and willpower across open ground.

Find the Postern Gate (Cunning or Cunning, modified [Resolute←15])

Hrafnborg's walls have a tannery waste-gate—a postern used to drain effluvium from the old tanneries. It should be there. Find it in the dark, in the mud, while things reach for you from every direction.

Signal the Wall (Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive, modified [Resolute←15])

Get the attention of the guards on the rampart without alerting the dead. Use the King's cipher, a lantern code, or pure desperate courage. The guards have been watching the siege ring for days—they're jumpy and exhausted.

Cover the Retreat (Quick or Discreet, modified [Resolute←15])

Stay behind, draw attention away from the group, then slip through at the last moment. The bait play. It requires confidence, speed, and trust that the gate will still be open when you get there.

Shield Kaelen (Might or Presence)

The ember-mark is flaring now—she can't suppress it fully. Put yourself physically between Kaelen and the reaching hands. The dead are aware of something warm. Keep them from finding out what.

Keep Lew Moving (Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant, modified [Resolute←15])

Lew is flagging—he's a finder of things, not a soldier, and twelve days of march have taken their toll. Keep him on his feet and moving forward when his legs say stop. The gate is close.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Narrative Interlude: Among the Dead

The party is inside the siege ring. The smell hits first—not rot, but cold. A deep, mineral cold, like the inside of a cave that has never seen sunlight. They stand among the dead, close enough to touch. A draugr in a farmer's clothes, jaw hanging loose, frost threading his beard. A woman in a burial shift, eyes blank white, hands curled around nothing. A child.

None of them look at the party.

Kaelen has her hand pressed over her forehead so hard her knuckles are white. The ember-mark pulses beneath her fingers like a second heartbeat.

"Keep. Moving," Lew hisses.

Combat Complication: The Watchers (between rounds)

A cluster of Frost-Ghouls—faster, hungrier dead circling the siege ring's second chance (two rolls, take best)—detect the party's warmth. They don't raise an alarm (the dead don't communicate that way), but they attack.

Constraint: This fight must be fast and quiet. If it drags past 3 rounds, the siege ring closes—2 Draugr Thralls shamble toward the fighting at the start of round 4 and each subsequent round. At round 6, the entire host turns—escape with consequences.

Enemies: 4 Frost-Ghouls (Level 2 Standard). Terrain: frozen marsh (difficult throughout), tannery channels (1-feet (2 m)-wide, 1 meters trenches of icy chemical runoff—fall in: 3 cold damage, restrained until climb out). No torches or lanterns—they draw the entire host. Without light, attacks have a second chance (two rolls, take worst) unless darkvision or similar.

Total Success

The party reaches the postern gate undetected. Guards open it immediately—they recognize the King's cipher. The group enters without additional combat. Earn 1 Victory.

Partial Success

The party reaches the gate, but the commotion draws a secondary wave of dead. They make it through, but one PC takes 5 damage from grasping hands, and the guards must seal the gate with fire and oil. Hrafnborg's defenses are weakened: −1 to the city's Defence Track.

Total Failure

The postern route collapses. The dead surge. The party fights a running battle to the main gate, where Harald orders a sortie to pull them in. Each PC takes 8 damage. The sortie costs lives—6 Hrafnborg guards die. Harald's anger and grief set the tone for the reunion scene. −2 to the city's Defence Track.

Defence Track Reference

10–8: Holding. Guards tired but disciplined. Walls intact. | 7–5: Strained. Sections need reinforcement. Rationing tightened. | 4–2: Critical. Breaches patched with rubble. Fires on the wall-walk. Civilian casualties. | 1–0: Falling. Dead inside the second ring. Evacuation to the Ember chamber.
